text: How do you do the dark notes? PLEASE ANSWER!
author: @JohnHisic

text:
+metayoshi1 In MPC the accepted method of using a staccato is the glitch where, if you try to place a note one note lower than the lowest it actually supports, on a beat that doesn't have any other notes yet, it'll create a bugged silent note that acts as a staccato for that instrument. Once you've placed whatever bugnotes you want to on a beat, you can then place normal notes as you please.

This isn't MPC, though. This is Super Mario Paint, which has an actual staccato function in the form of the grey notes: https://github.com/DC37/Super-Mario-Paint/releases
author: @MediaMunkee

text: +John Hisic Think it has something to do with the note or instrument X buttons. Mainly used to black them out. Some people say it's a glitch of the newest version of MPC, but I doubt that's possible. We know that shift and control keys change the pitch of the note, but perhaps ALT causes them to be blacked out. I need to look for this though, I haven't seen this kind of MPC before.
author: @metayoshi1712

text: ah. good to know. I already found it beforehand.
author: @metayoshi1712

text: +MediaMunkee Or wait maybe you're trying to load the songs from the arrangements screen? You actually have to go to the song screen, load a song, switch to the arrangement screen, and click the Add button there. Not the most intuitive.
